process_notes: Before each new valid submission, checked today's remaining submissions and latest Kaggle Code updates.
Discussion searches did not reveal an actionable new 0.87 direction during this cycle.
Evaluation queue latency was high: later submissions waited roughly one hour before scoring.
All 5 requested valid submissions completed; the extra Matthew ERROR was not counted.
direction_notes: The strongest reproducible public score remains 0.86, not 0.87.
Fresh or repackaged Kien-family controls usually landed at 0.85-0.86.
Low-score fresh training outputs should not be submitted just because they are recent RTX6000 runs.
Next real improvement direction should be a verified new adapter or targeted reasoning-category surgery, not repeated safe control submissions.
